comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> really impressive to see how more and more things are reported with each bump of the linter(s)

impressive yes, but I could argue it is a failure of the ecosystem if so many linters are needed to catch bugs and performance problems.

> impressive yes, but I could argue it is a failure of the ecosystem if so many linters are needed to catch bugs and performance problems.

What would be the alternative?  I recall LLVM getting better on each release as well.  Writing such static analyzers is quite difficult and sometimes requires expensive compiler techniques/transformations.

I don't want to get into this discussion right now and I am not saying I know better, there is no such things as the one right way. All I say I prefer the compiler to prevent me from compiling known buggy code instead of having to rely on third party linters to that.
